name: Cocker Spaniel
description: The American Cocker Spaniel is the smallest of the AKC Sporting Group, developed as a specialist bird dog for hunting woodcock and small upland birds, and was the most popular breed in America for a record 23 consecutive years. Their long silky coats, soulful dark eyes, and constantly wagging tails give them enormous appeal as family companions, equally at home in the field or on the sofa. Long floppy ears trap moisture and debris, requiring weekly cleaning to prevent the chronic ear infections to which the breed is especially predisposed.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy (PRA)
conditions_detail.description: PRA is a group of hereditary diseases causing progressive degeneration of the photoreceptor cells in the retina. It leads to night blindness first, followed by complete blindness. There is no treatment, but DNA tests exist for many forms.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: There is no effective treatment or cure for PRA. Management focuses on adapting the dog's environment to maintain quality of life as vision deteriorates.
prevention_tips: DNA test breeding stock before mating
Only breed clear or carrier-to-clear pairings

conditions_detail.name: Cherry Eye (Prolapsed Nictitating Membrane Gland)
conditions_detail.description: Cherry eye is the prolapse of the tear-producing gland of the third eyelid, appearing as a red or pink mass in the inner corner of the eye. It is more common in brachycephalic and young breeds, and can affect one or both eyes.
symptoms: Red or pink mass in inner eye corner
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Surgical replacement (tucking or pocket technique) of the gland back into its normal position is preferred over removal to preserve tear production. Surgery success rate is high.
prevention_tips: Early surgical correction prevents secondary dry eye
